verses.text: 'Bless the LORD, O my soul,' David urges himself, and 'forget not all his benefits.' He stirs his own heart to praise. Gratitude must be roused, lest we take God's mercies for granted.
'Bless the LORD, O my soul,' David urges himself, and 'forget not all his benefits.' He stirs his own heart to praise. Gratitude must be roused, lest we take God's mercies for granted.

God forgives all iniquities, heals diseases, redeems life from destruction, and crowns with lovingkindness, renewing youth like the eagle's. His benefits are full and free. Pardon, healing, rescue, and renewal all flow from His hand.

God forgives all iniquities, heals diseases, redeems life from destruction, and crowns with lovingkindness, renewing youth like the eagle's. His benefits are full and free. Pardon, healing, rescue, and renewal all flow from His hand.

God forgives all iniquities, heals diseases, redeems life from destruction, and crowns with lovingkindness, renewing youth like the eagle's. His benefits are full and free. Pardon, healing, rescue, and renewal all flow from His hand.

The LORD executes righteousness for the oppressed and is 'merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and plenteous in mercy.' His character is kindness itself. The God who judges the wrong is rich in patience toward the weak.

The LORD executes righteousness for the oppressed and is 'merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and plenteous in mercy.' His character is kindness itself. The God who judges the wrong is rich in patience toward the weak.

The LORD executes righteousness for the oppressed and is 'merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and plenteous in mercy.' His character is kindness itself. The God who judges the wrong is rich in patience toward the weak.

He will not always chide nor keep His anger, nor deal with us after our sins, for 'as far as the east is from the west' He removes our transgressions. Pardon is complete. God puts our sins at an immeasurable distance, never to meet us again.

He will not always chide nor keep His anger, nor deal with us after our sins, for 'as far as the east is from the west' He removes our transgressions. Pardon is complete. God puts our sins at an immeasurable distance, never to meet us again.

He will not always chide nor keep His anger, nor deal with us after our sins, for 'as far as the east is from the west' He removes our transgressions. Pardon is complete. God puts our sins at an immeasurable distance, never to meet us again.

He will not always chide nor keep His anger, nor deal with us after our sins, for 'as far as the east is from the west' He removes our transgressions. Pardon is complete. God puts our sins at an immeasurable distance, never to meet us again.

'Like as a father pitieth his children,' so the LORD pities those who fear Him, for 'he knoweth our frame; he remembereth that we are dust.' His tenderness reckons our weakness. God deals with us gently because He knows how frail we are.

'Like as a father pitieth his children,' so the LORD pities those who fear Him, for 'he knoweth our frame; he remembereth that we are dust.' His tenderness reckons our weakness. God deals with us gently because He knows how frail we are.
Man's days are as grass and a flower of the field, gone when the wind passes over. Our life is fleeting. The brevity of man only magnifies the enduring mercy that follows.
Man's days are as grass and a flower of the field, gone when the wind passes over. Our life is fleeting. The brevity of man only magnifies the enduring mercy that follows.

But the LORD's mercy is 'from everlasting to everlasting upon them that fear him,' and His righteousness to their children. His love outlasts our short lives. The covenant mercy of God reaches across the generations.

But the LORD's mercy is 'from everlasting to everlasting upon them that fear him,' and His righteousness to their children. His love outlasts our short lives. The covenant mercy of God reaches across the generations.

God has set His throne in the heavens, ruling over all; so let angels, hosts, and all His works bless Him, and David ends, 'bless the LORD, O my soul.' Heaven and earth are called to praise. The psalm closes as it began, with the soul stirred to bless God.
